expected_artifacts.description: Expected artifacts and their required-or-optional status.

approval_policy.description: Describes which actions require explicit approval before execution.

cost_limit.description: Maximum allowed cost (e.g., USD, tokens, API calls).

model_visibility_policy.description: Whether the participant model identity must be reported.

transcript_policy.description: Whether and how the participant transcript should be preserved for judging.

tier.description: Verification tier indicating readiness for competitive use. draft: exists but untested. smoke: runs through harness on at least one adapter. verified: human or trusted baseline agent completed it and the judge result matched the intended rubric. retired: withdrawn due to leakage, ambiguity, environment drift, or evaluator mismatch.

baseline.description: Human or trusted baseline record for this task. Populated when the task reaches smoke or verified tier.

baseline_actor.description: Human name, adapter identifier, or reference runner who completed the baseline run.

baseline_time_sec.description: Baseline completion wall-clock time in seconds.

baseline_success.description: Whether the baseline run was completed successfully.

baseline_block_reason.description: Explanation of why the baseline was blocked, if not successful.

baseline_artifact_ref.description: Path or URL to the baseline result packet, evidence bundle, or run identifier.

difficulty_notes.description: Observations about what made the task easy or hard during baseline execution.

known_ambiguities.description: Wording concerns, evaluator drift, or environment sensitivities discovered during baseline.

mismatch_issue_refs.description: Links to GitHub or tracker issues recording mismatches discovered during baseline. Mismatches must be filed as issues rather than silently editing history.
